description: To establish connection or relevance between things, people, or ideas. 'Phiremo' names the act of drawing relationships—showing how one thing connects to another, how people stand in relation, how ideas link and illuminate each other.
sound_symbolism: The word connects through breath and flow. 'Phi' /ɸi/ begins with soft aspiration, reaching toward connection. Then 're' rolls with extending warmth, relationship forming. Finally 'mo' settles with nasal closeness, relation established.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mia thia phiremo.' (I relate to you.) Can describe conceptual connection: 'ha thena ra thena phiremo.' (This thing relates to that thing.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: All things exist in relationship—nothing stands alone. 'Phiremo' names our capacity to perceive and articulate these connections.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Relating to others—finding common ground and shared experience—is the foundation of empathy and understanding.
